name: Samsung Ballie
short_description: A bowling-ball-sized rolling home robot with a built-in projector and AI assistant that autonomously navigates the house and manages connected appliances.
long_description: Ballie is a spherical, self-driving domestic robot that uses a spatial LiDAR sensor to roam the home and a dual-lens 1080p projector to display video calls, movies, workout guides, and greetings onto walls and floors. It connects to and orchestrates smart-home devices, monitors pets and family, and learns user routines to deliver personalized help. First shown as a concept at CES 2020 and revamped at CES 2024, Ballie matters as a mainstream consumer attempt to make an ambient, mobile AI a cohabiting presence in everyday domestic life rather than a fixed appliance.

images.caption: Samsung Ballie, the yellow spherical AI companion robot, beside its projector on a table at CES 2024.
images.alt_text: A small yellow spherical robot sits on a wooden table next to a projector unit in a home setting with a bookshelf in the background.

sources.title: A Day in the Life With Ballie: An AI Companion Robot for the Home
sources.access_notes: Official Samsung Newsroom announcement of the CES 2024 Ballie.

sources.title: Samsung brings back Ballie, its home robot, at CES 2024 — with a few upgrades
sources.access_notes: Reports bowling-ball size, spatial LiDAR, dual-lens 1080p projector, 2-3 hour battery.
